what_it_does: Declares a planned mobile surface specification for view G3, listing the touch interactions it represents (drag, zoom), the addressable UI targets it maps to, and the build outputs and components it depends on.
observations: Generated surface-spec with metadata id G3, area G, title, platforms mobile, and status planned.
home_journey is "_unrouted" and both journeys and cross_journeys are empty arrays.
represents two capability names: CAN-drag and CAN-zoom.
addresses lists three UI resources: ui://canvas/drag, ui://canvas/zoomhint, and ui://toolbar/fit.
build_to names components canvas and toolbar, a design_lint command, and a tokens source.
Includes a self-describing provenance note that it was built by a Claude Code session and must not invent addresses.
standouts: Journeys and cross_journeys are empty even though the file is meant to carry the web B3.
Status is planned, so the surface exists as a declaration without routed integration.
Explicit address list ties the surface to canvas and toolbar UI targets.
conventions: Follows a JSON surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses ui:// URI-style identifiers for addressable UI resources.
Places un-routed generated specs in an _unrouted directory separate from the journey spine.
Records build-time dependencies including design lint and token generation.
concerns: journeys and cross_journeys are empty, so the spec's connection to the journey spine is not yet realized.
represents uses capability names CAN-drag and CAN-zoom without defining them in this file.
build_to.design_lint is a shell-like command string with an open fail-on placeholder; exact invocation is unclear.
notes: A generated, un-routed mobile surface spec for canvas touch interactions that currently declares target addresses and build dependencies but has no linked journeys.
questions: What determines when this surface moves from _unrouted to a concrete journey?
How are CAN-drag and CAN-zoom validated or mapped to the listed addresses?
Is the design_lint command a literal command or a template to be filled in?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a generated surface-spec for view G3; actual content matches, but it is still un-routed with empty journey references.
apparent_themes: Separation of generated specs from routed journey spine
Build-time design-system gating via lint and tokens
intention_signals: It is trying to capture a mobile canvas touch-interaction surface, map it to specific UI addresses and components, and prepare it for integration into the journey spine once routed.
novelty: Explicitly marks itself as generated and prohibits invented addresses, while keeping journey references empty in an _unrouted directory.
contribution: Provides a concrete, addressable surface definition for mobile canvas drag and zoom interactions, including the UI targets and build-time design-system hooks that a fused system could link to journeys and components.
summary_for_embedding: A planned, generated JSON surface specification for mobile canvas touch interactions (G3) that references canvas and toolbar UI addresses and declares build-time lint and token dependencies while remaining un-routed.
intention_for_embedding: To record the intended mobile touch surface for the canvas before it is assigned to journeys and cross-journeys in the design blueprint.
